modules.module_title: Introduction to Eldercare, Aging Process & Psychology of Elderly
Daily Living Support (ADLs), Hygiene, Mobility & Transfers
Nutrition, Hydration, Special Diets & Medication Management
Vital Signs Monitoring, Common Geriatric Illnesses & Chronic Conditions
Safety, Fall Prevention, First Aid & Emergency Response
Communication, Empathy, Dementia/Alzheimer Care & Professional Boundaries

questions.question: Elder Care Companion (vrdhh-jan dekhbhal saathi) ka primary role kya hota hai?

options.A: Elderly client ko emotional companionship, daily activities (ADLs) me assist karna aur safe lifestyle maintain karna
options.C: Diagnosing illness and prescribing high-dose medicines independently

questions.explanation: Elder care companion elderly people ko daily life support, physical help, empathy aur lifestyle safety checks systems me companion support deta hai.

questions.question: Elderly people me age ke sath sensory organs (aankh, kaan) changes me 'Presbycusis' kis problem ko kehte hain?

options.A: Age-related gradual hearing loss (sunne ki shakti kam hona)
options.B: Loss of visual reading capabilities directly

questions.explanation: Presbycusis age-related gradual hearing degradation ko kehte hain, isliye companion ko unse dhire aur clear voice pitch me aamne-saamne baate karni chahiye.

questions.question: Daily Living Activities (ADLs) me elderly client ko bed se wheelchair par transfer karte waqt companion ke liye safest body posture kya hona chahiye?

options.A: Keep back straight and bend knees (knees ke force se lift karna, avoiding back strain)
options.B: Bending back sideways with high sudden pull forces
options.C: Lifting client single-handedly without locked breaks on wheelchair

questions.explanation: Knees bend karke straight back se lift karne par body gravity centers use hotay hain jo companion ko back disc injuries se protect karte hain aur client transfer safe banate hain.

questions.question: Elderly clients me 'Pressure Sores / Bedsores' (bed ke zakhm) kis vajah se bante hain?

options.A: Prolonged static pressure on bony areas (jaise heel, hips) due to staying in bed in same position for hours

questions.explanation: Bedridden patients me dynamic weight movements zero hone par bony spots par blood circulation stop ho jata hai, jisse bedsores bante hain. Prevention: turn position every 2 hours.

questions.question: Dementia (smriti-bhram) ya Alzheimer's disease se beemar elderly client ke sath communication style kya honi chahiye?

options.A: Simple, short sentences use karna, patient tone maintain rakhna aur client ko continuous correct karne se bachna
options.B: Arguing with client over lost memories loudly

questions.explanation: Alzheimer's patients fast speech and corrections se quickly frustrate ho jate hain, short clear statements and validation empathy comfort provide karte hain.

questions.question: Geriatric medicine safety me 'Polypharmacy' term kya represent karti hai?

options.A: Single client ko multiple daily prescription drugs serve hona (increases side effects and drug interaction risks)

questions.explanation: Polypharmacy (typically 5+ daily drugs) elderly people me organs weakness, confusing behaviors and falls ka major cause banta hai, close medication logs tracking mandatory hai.

questions.question: Elderly clients home safety audits checks me room floors falls prevention me sabse zaroori physical adjustments kya hai?

options.A: Remove loose rugs/carpets, install grab bars in toilets and keep floors dry and free of clutter wire pathways
options.B: Placing glossy wax polish on entire floor hamesha
options.C: Keeping rooms dark at night to save electricity

questions.explanation: Falls are leading cause of bone fractures (eg hip fracture) in elderly, floor safety grab-bars installation reduces slip events dramatically.

questions.question: Diabetes (madhumeh) se beemar elderly client ke daily foot care (pairon ki dekhbhal) checks me kya inspect karna chahiye companion ko?

options.A: Check daily for redness, small blisters, cuts or sensory numbness (diabetes slows wound healing, risking gangrene)
options.B: Rinsing feet with hot boiling water directly always
options.C: Only cutting nails to root skins

questions.explanation: Diabetic neuropathy pain signals block karti hai. Foot cuts/blisters unnoticed rehne par severe ulcers construct ho jate hain, daily visual check mandatory checks hai.

questions.question: Hypertension (high blood pressure) tracking me vital signs monitoring me ideal systolic/diastolic blood pressure reading margins for healthy adult range?

options.A: 120/80 mmHg

questions.explanation: Standard blood pressure average base 120/80 mmHg range checks, variations exceeding limits require logging alerts.

questions.question: Elderly client me sudden chest tightness, left arm pain and difficulty breathing emergency signs kis health crisis ko alert karte hain?

options.A: Heart Attack emergency (hri-dayaghaat - demands immediate ambulance call)

questions.explanation: Arm radiation pain and chest pressure are class cardiac crisis markers. Immediate emergency medical response is mandatory.

questions.question: Elder care companion professional ethics checks me cash handling or bank cards usage rules parents/clients ke sath kya hain?

options.A: Never use client cards or handle cash without written parental/family ledger approvals and billing record proof logs
options.B: Handle client money freely without records
options.C: Charge secret commissions directly from client wallet

questions.explanation: Strict financial bookkeeping ledger entries protect companions from false allegations of theft or financial abuse.

questions.question: Dehydration (paani ki kami) checks me elderly clients ka thirst sensation (pyas lagne ka bhaan) age ke sath kya morph hota hai?

options.A: Decreases drastically (elderly people feel less thirsty even when body is dry, demanding scheduled hourly water reminders)

questions.explanation: Hypothalamus sensitivity drops with age. Companion scheduled water pushes block urinary tract infections (UTIs) due to dehydration.

questions.question: Wheelchair locking mechanisms setups checks did wheelchair brakes must be locked during client transfer inputs?

options.A: Yes, always (unlocked wheels slide backward during patient sitting weight drops, causing tail bone fractures)
options.B: No, wheels must spin free for flexibility

questions.explanation: Securing wheel brakes provides rigid platform stability during weight shifts, preventing slipping accidents.

questions.question: Elderly client cognitive health exercises me 'Reminiscence Therapy' (purani yaadein taaza karna) how it is executed?

options.A: Browsing old photo albums, talking about client childhood memories, and playing songs from their youth eras
options.C: Forcing client to remember today's news headlines repeatedly

questions.explanation: Reminiscence uses long-term memory tracks (often preserved in dementia) to stimulate comfort emotions and positive self worth.

questions.question: Companion business documentation templates 'Medication Log Sheet' why essential checklist?

options.A: Tracks date/time of drugs administration, dose counts, and signatures, preventing accidental double dosing mistakes

questions.explanation: Medication compliance logging protects client health, avoiding toxic drug duplications or missed critical therapies.

options.A: Elderly client ko emotional companionship, daily activities (ADLs) me assist karna aur safe lifestyle maintain karna
options.C: Diagnosing illness and prescribing high-dose medicines independently

questions.explanation: Elder care companion elderly people ko daily life support, physical help, empathy aur lifestyle safety checks systems me companion support deta hai.

options.B: Loss of visual reading capabilities directly

questions.explanation: Presbycusis age-related gradual hearing degradation ko kehte hain, isliye companion ko unse dhire aur clear voice pitch me aamne-saamne baate karni chahiye.

questions.explanation: Knees bend karke straight back se lift karne par body gravity centers use hotay hain jo companion ko back disc injuries se protect karte hain aur client transfer safe banate hain.

questions.explanation: Bedridden patients me dynamic weight movements zero hone par bony spots par blood circulation stop ho jata hai, jisse bedsores bante hain. Prevention: turn position every 2 hours.

options.B: Handle client money freely without records
options.C: Charge secret commissions directly from client wallet

questions.explanation: Hypothalamus sensitivity drops with age. Companion scheduled water pushes block urinary tract infections (UTIs) due to dehydration.

options.A: Always (unlocked wheels slide backward during patient sitting weight drops, causing tail bone fractures)
options.B: No, wheels must spin free for flexibility

questions.explanation: Securing wheel brakes provides rigid platform stability during weight shifts, preventing slipping accidents.

questions.question: Cognitive Reminiscence old photo?

options.A: Browsing old photo albums, talking about client childhood memories, and playing songs from their youth eras
options.C: Forcing client to remember today's news headlines repeatedly

questions.question: Companion medication log?

options.A: Tracks date/time of drugs administration, dose counts, and signatures, preventing accidental double dosing mistakes

questions.explanation: Medication compliance logging protects client health, avoiding toxic drug duplications or missed critical therapies.

questions.question: Elderly mobility checks 'Gait belt / Transfer belt' why locked around client waist during walking assistance?

options.A: Provides secure physical handle grip for companion to catch client if they lose balance or trip
options.B: Gait belts help straighten bones automatically

questions.explanation: Gait belts provide leverage grips, helping companions stabilize unsteady clients without pulling or injuring their arms/shoulders.

questions.question: Elderly vital signs checks 'Pulse Oximeter' what physiological metrics does it evaluate?

options.A: Oxygen saturation percentage in blood (SpO2) and heart pulse rate

questions.explanation: Pulse oximeters clamp on fingers, checking respiratory oxygen deliveries. Normal ranges are typically 95-100% SpO2.

questions.question: Geriatric conditions 'Osteoporosis' (haddi kamzori) safety guidelines why gentle physical handlings are required?

options.A: Bones are highly fragile (severe osteoporosis bone structures can fracture from minor impacts or firm grips)
options.B: Osteoporosis causes sudden blood pressure drop

questions.explanation: Osteoporosis decreases bone density. Caregivers use gentle, supportive lifts, avoiding sudden pulls or jerks.

questions.question: Alzheimer's care setups 'Sundowning effect' (suraj dhalne par confusion badhna) typical behaviors?

options.A: Increased confusion, agitation, pacing and wandering behaviors in late afternoon/evening hours

questions.explanation: Sundowning relates to fatigue and biological clock disruptions. Keeping rooms bright in evening and maintaining quiet routines diffuses sundown agitation.

questions.question: Elder care home setups 'Bathroom Safety' why non-slip shower chairs are installed inside tubs?

options.A: Allows unsteady elderly clients to sit safely during bathing, reducing slips under shower runs

questions.explanation: Standing on wet soapy floors is highly hazardous for unsteady geriatric clients. Shower benches provide stable, sitting bath security.

questions.question: Elderly mobility gait belt?

options.A: Provides secure physical handle grip for companion to catch client if they lose balance or trip
options.B: Gait belts help straighten bones automatically

questions.explanation: Gait belts provide leverage grips, helping companions stabilize unsteady clients without pulling or injuring their arms/shoulders.

questions.question: Elderly vital signs pulse oximeter?

options.A: Oxygen saturation percentage in blood (SpO2) and heart pulse rate

questions.explanation: Pulse oximeters clamp on fingers, checking respiratory oxygen deliveries. Normal ranges are typically 95-100% SpO2.

options.A: Bones are highly fragile (severe osteoporosis bone structures can fracture from minor impacts or firm grips)
options.B: Osteoporosis causes sudden blood pressure drop

questions.explanation: Osteoporosis decreases bone density. Caregivers use gentle, supportive lifts, avoiding sudden pulls or jerks.

options.A: Increased confusion, agitation, pacing and wandering behaviors in late afternoon/evening hours

questions.explanation: Sundowning relates to fatigue and biological clock disruptions. Keeping rooms bright in evening and maintaining quiet routines diffuses sundown agitation.

questions.question: Elder care home bathroom chair?

options.A: Allows unsteady elderly clients to sit safely during bathing, reducing slips under shower runs

questions.explanation: Standing on wet soapy floors is highly hazardous for unsteady geriatric clients. Shower benches provide stable, sitting bath security.

questions.question: Elderly nutrition 'Dysphagia' (nigalne me takleef) adjustments in meals consistency?

options.A: Thickening liquids, pureeing solid foods and avoiding thin liquids to prevent choking aspiration lung infections
options.B: Serving raw hard celery sticks and whole nuts

questions.explanation: Dysphagia triggers aspiration (liquids slipping into airways causing pneumonia). Thickened liquids and pureed foods are standard clinical dietary adjustments.

questions.question: Vital signs checks 'Fever margins' which temperature indicates high fever in geriatric clients?

options.A: 101.3 F (38.5 C) or higher (demands immediate logging and notifying medical contact)

questions.explanation: Geriatric clients show blunted fever responses; even slight temperature rises indicate serious underlying infections (eg pneumonia/UTI).

questions.question: Geriatric illnesses 'Dementia Wandering risk' safety proofing home doors what is recommended?

options.A: Install safety door alarms or place deadbolts high above eye-level (wandering dementia clients rarely look up for keys)
options.B: Leave main doors unlocked and open
options.C: Locking client inside small bathroom closets

questions.explanation: Dementia clients may wander into streets, risking trauma. High deadbolt positioning secures doors safely out of visual focus lines.

questions.question: Geriatric daily checks 'Hydration status' does dark yellow urine indicate what status?

options.A: Dehydration (requires immediate scheduled fluid intake pushes, unless fluid restrictions apply)

questions.explanation: Urine color concentrations are visual proxies for hydration levels. Clear/pale yellow is target healthy hydration.

questions.question: Elder care companion business setups 'Service Agreement contracts' why client medical history sheets are collected?

options.A: Documents chronic conditions, allergy warnings, emergency contact lists and physician phone numbers for instant safety access

questions.explanation: Emergency medical sheets on files ensure rapid, correct clinical details transfers during health crises.

questions.question: Elderly nutrition Dysphagia?

options.A: Thickening liquids, pureeing solid foods and avoiding thin liquids to prevent choking aspiration lung infections
options.B: Serving raw hard celery sticks and whole nuts

questions.explanation: Dysphagia triggers aspiration (liquids slipping into airways causing pneumonia). Thickened liquids and pureed foods are standard clinical dietary adjustments.

questions.explanation: Geriatric clients show blunted fever responses; even slight temperature rises indicate serious underlying infections (eg pneumonia/UTI).

options.B: Leave main doors unlocked and open
options.C: Locking client inside small bathroom closets

questions.explanation: Dementia clients may wander into streets, risking trauma. High deadbolt positioning secures doors safely out of visual focus lines.

questions.question: Elder care service contracts?

options.A: Documents chronic conditions, allergy warnings, emergency contact lists and physician phone numbers for instant safety access

questions.explanation: Emergency medical sheets on files ensure rapid, correct clinical details transfers during health crises.

questions.question: Elder care mobility assist 'Walker safety check' why rubber tips of walker legs must be checked weekly?

options.A: Worn-out rubber tips lose floor grip friction, causing walker to slip slip under client weight
options.B: Tip wear alters walker height levels

questions.explanation: Worn rubber pads expose smooth metal pipes, sliding on tile floors and causing high falls risks.

questions.question: Geriatric nutrition 'Low Sodium diet' why recommended for heart failure/hypertension clients?

options.A: Restricting salt reduces water retention fluid loads, protecting heart and lowering arterial blood pressure

questions.explanation: High sodium pulls water into bloodstreams, overloading weak heart ventricles. Low-salt diets stabilize blood pressure.

questions.question: Elderly transfer safety 'Bed to Stand pivot' where client feet must be placed before assisting to stand?

options.A: Flat on floor, slightly apart, with non-skid socks/shoes on (provides stable foundation support)
options.B: Dangling loose in air off bed margins

questions.explanation: Flat foot contact with non-slip shoes ensures grip stability, preventing knee collapses or foot slips.

questions.question: Geriatric health checks 'UTI behavioral symptoms' why sudden acute confusion alerts UTI in elderly?

options.A: Urinary infections release toxins causing delirium/confusion in seniors, rather than classic painful burning symptoms
options.B: Confusion means patient has permanent dementia always

questions.explanation: Aged immune paths respond differently. Sudden confusion (delirium) is classic masking indicator of urinary tract infections.

questions.question: Elder care companion communication 'Active Listening' how to respond to client repeating same story?

options.A: Listen with patience, acknowledge feelings, and validate story value (do not interrupt or say 'you told me this')
options.B: Interrupt and correct their memory immediately
options.C: Leave room as soon as story starts
options.D: Write stories down in reports only

questions.explanation: Validation therapies provide dignity comfort, whereas correction triggers panic and agitation in memory-lacking seniors.

questions.question: Elder care walker safety?

options.A: Worn-out rubber tips lose floor grip friction, causing walker to slip slip under client weight
options.B: Tip wear alters walker height levels

questions.explanation: Worn rubber pads expose smooth metal pipes, sliding on tile floors and causing high falls risks.

questions.question: Geriatric nutrition low sodium?

options.A: Restricting salt reduces water retention fluid loads, protecting heart and lowering arterial blood pressure

questions.explanation: High sodium pulls water into bloodstreams, overloading weak heart ventricles. Low-salt diets stabilize blood pressure.

questions.question: Elderly transfer feet placement?

options.A: Flat on floor, slightly apart, with non-skid socks/shoes on (provides stable foundation support)
options.B: Dangling loose in air off bed margins

questions.explanation: Flat foot contact with non-slip shoes ensures grip stability, preventing knee collapses or foot slips.

options.B: Confusion means patient has permanent dementia always

questions.question: Elder care communication active?

options.A: Listen with patience, acknowledge feelings, and validate story value (do not interrupt or say 'you told me this')
options.B: Interrupt and correct their memory immediately
options.C: Leave room as soon as story starts
options.D: Write stories down in reports only

questions.explanation: Validation therapies provide dignity comfort, whereas correction triggers panic and agitation in memory-lacking seniors.

questions.question: Geriatric mobility support 'Transfer board / Slide board' why used in transfers?

options.A: Bridges gap between bed and wheelchair, allowing client to slide across sitting down without standing up
options.B: Slide boards are exercise fitness boards

questions.explanation: Slide boards enable sitting transfers for paralyzed/weak leg seniors, protecting caregiver shoulders from strain.

questions.question: Seniors hygiene 'Shower temperature controls' what is maximum safe water temperature?

options.A: 100 F to 105 F (37-40 C - senior skin is thin and easily scalded/burned by hot water)

questions.explanation: Thin aging skin loses fat layers, making it highly vulnerable to severe scalding burns at hot temps.

questions.question: Elderly client behavior 'Repetitive asking' (eg asking time repeatedly) how companion reacts?

options.A: Answer calmly, write time on large white clock board visually, and redirect to engaging activity
options.D: Keep repeating exact answers for hours loudly

questions.explanation: Visual cues (white boards) support short memory limits, while activity redirection breaks repetition loops.

questions.question: Home safety proofing 'Tub grab bar installation' where grab bars must be bolted?

options.A: Studs behind wall boards (must withstand direct heavy weight load pulls, not glued to tiles)
options.B: Glued directly onto shiny tiles surfaces only

questions.explanation: Glues fail under body weight pulls. Screwing grab bars into wooden wall studs guarantees weight-bearing fall safety.

questions.question: Companion business bookkeeping variable expenses what is variable cost in eldercare services?

options.A: Travel fuel tickets, medical gloves supplies, and customized meal raw material purchases

questions.explanation: Variable costs scale with care frequency and clients counts, directly impact services gross profit margins.

questions.question: Geriatric mobility slide board?

options.A: Bridges gap between bed and wheelchair, allowing client to slide across sitting down without standing up
options.B: Slide boards are exercise fitness boards

questions.explanation: Slide boards enable sitting transfers for paralyzed/weak leg seniors, protecting caregiver shoulders from strain.

questions.question: Elderly physical checks 'Postural Hypotension' (blood pressure drop on standing) what safety action is required?

options.A: Have client sit on bed edge dangling legs for 2 minutes before standing up to prevent dizziness falls
options.B: Have client jump off bed rapidly
options.C: Give high dose caffeine pills before standing

questions.explanation: Dangling legs allows blood circulation pressures to stabilize, avoiding sudden orthostatic brain drops leading to faints.

questions.question: Geriatric care 'Choking rescue in wheelchair' how Heimlich maneuver is modified for sitting clients?

options.A: Stand behind wheelchair, wrap arms around client midriff, pull upward inward thrusts below ribs
options.B: Tilt wheelchair backward to floor directly
options.C: Lay client flat on stomach across desk
options.D: Heimlich cannot be done in wheelchair

questions.explanation: Standard Heimlich sitting wraps adapt upward compression direction, clearing windpipe blocks safely without wheel-tipping hazards.

questions.question: Elder care safety proofing 'Water temperature limiters' (mixing valves) why set at water heaters?

options.A: Restricts hot tap water maximum output to 120 F (49 C), preventing instant skin scalding burns
options.B: Reduces gas heating fuel bills to zero

questions.explanation: Seniors have slower reflexes and thin skin. Limiting tap temperature prevents accidental hot water burns.

questions.question: Seniors health monitoring 'Fecal Impaction' signs checks?

options.A: Absence of bowel movements for days, liquid stool leaks, abdominal bloating pain and nausea

questions.explanation: Severe chronic constipation leads to impaction blocks, requiring rapid nurse notifications and medical interventions.

questions.question: Elder care companion business contracts 'Incident log entries' what is the reporting timeframe standard after emergency?

options.A: Immediate report creation (log and notify family within 24 hours of occurrence)
options.C: Report only if client demands audit

questions.explanation: Documenting incidents immediately ensures clean record compliance, shielding companions from legal disputes.

questions.question: Elderly postural hypotension?

options.A: Have client sit on bed edge dangling legs for 2 minutes before standing up to prevent dizziness falls
options.B: Have client jump off bed rapidly
options.C: Give high dose caffeine pills before standing

questions.explanation: Dangling legs allows blood circulation pressures to stabilize, avoiding sudden orthostatic brain drops leading to faints.

options.D: Heimlich cannot be done in wheelchair

questions.explanation: Standard Heimlich sitting wraps adapt upward compression direction, clearing windpipe blocks safely without wheel-tipping hazards.

questions.question: Elder care incident report timeline?

options.A: Immediate report creation (log and notify family within 24 hours of occurrence)
options.C: Report only if client demands audit

questions.explanation: Documenting incidents immediately ensures clean record compliance, shielding companions from legal disputes.

questions.question: Seniors sleep hygiene 'Sleep Apnea warning signs' what is standard indicator?

options.A: Loud chronic snoring, gasping for air during sleep, and daytime excessive sleepiness

questions.explanation: Sleep apnea stops breathing cycles temporarily, leading to cardiac strain. Snoring/gasping demands professional diagnostic CPAP evaluations.

questions.question: Elderly client cognitive exercises 'Crossword puzzles / Memory matches' what is main cognitive purpose?

options.A: Maintains neuro-plasticity, delays cognitive drops, and keeps memory tracks active
options.B: Passes time when caregiver is asleep

questions.explanation: Brain workout activities build neural reserves, supporting daily cognitive functionalities in aging seniors.

questions.question: Home safety check 'Ramp slope ratio' standard safe incline ratio for wheelchair ramps?

options.A: 1:12 slope ratio (1 inch of vertical rise requires 12 inches of ramp length to prevent tipping)
options.D: Ramps are illegal in residential zones

questions.explanation: Steep ramps lead to backward wheelchair tip-overs or slide crashes. 1:12 slope ensures safe manual pushes.

questions.question: Geriatric vital signs checks 'Symptom tracking logs' why daily temperature logging is performed?

options.A: Allows early detection of low-grade infections (seniors rarely develop high fevers early due to low immunity)

questions.explanation: Minor temperature changes indicate critical hidden infections (eg UTI/sepsis) in elderly, demanding rapid care tracking.

questions.question: Elder care companion business 'Indemnity insurance' why coverage is critical for companions?

options.A: Protects companions from financial liabilities if client claims injuries or falls during transfer assists

questions.explanation: Indemnity policies secure companions from devastating legal lawsuits related to patient transfer mishaps.

questions.question: Seniors sleep apnea?

options.A: Loud chronic snoring, gasping for air during sleep, and daytime excessive sleepiness

questions.explanation: Sleep apnea stops breathing cycles temporarily, leading to cardiac strain. Snoring/gasping demands professional diagnostic CPAP evaluations.

questions.question: Elderly client crossword?

options.A: Maintains neuro-plasticity, delays cognitive drops, and keeps memory tracks active
options.B: Passes time when caregiver is asleep

questions.explanation: Brain workout activities build neural reserves, supporting daily cognitive functionalities in aging seniors.

questions.question: Home safety ramp slope?

options.A: 1:12 slope ratio (1 inch of vertical rise requires 12 inches of ramp length to prevent tipping)
options.D: Ramps are illegal in residential zones

questions.explanation: Steep ramps lead to backward wheelchair tip-overs or slide crashes. 1:12 slope ensures safe manual pushes.

questions.question: Geriatric vital signs temperature?

options.A: Allows early detection of low-grade infections (seniors rarely develop high fevers early due to low immunity)

questions.explanation: Minor temperature changes indicate critical hidden infections (eg UTI/sepsis) in elderly, demanding rapid care tracking.

questions.question: Elder care indemnity?

options.A: Protects companions from financial liabilities if client claims injuries or falls during transfer assists

questions.explanation: Indemnity policies secure companions from devastating legal lawsuits related to patient transfer mishaps.

questions.question: Elder Care Companion final launch audits why CPR and First Aid verification is key final step?

options.A: Certifies competence in handling cardiac arrests and choking emergencies, protecting elderly client life safety

questions.explanation: Up-to-date emergency first responder certifications validate companion safety credentials, establishing hiring trusts.

questions.question: Elderly client sensory hearing loss?

options.A: Age-related gradual hearing loss (sunne ki shakti kam hona)
options.B: Loss of visual reading capabilities directly

questions.explanation: Presbycusis age-related gradual hearing degradation ko kehte hain, isliye companion ko unse dhire aur clear voice pitch me aamne-saamne baate karni chahiye.

questions.question: Daily Living Transfer safety back straight?

options.A: Keep back straight and bend knees (knees ke force se lift karna, avoiding back strain)
options.B: Bending back sideways with high sudden pull forces
options.C: Lifting client single-handedly without locked breaks on wheelchair

questions.explanation: Knees bend karke straight back se lift karne par body gravity centers use hotay hain jo companion ko back disc injuries se protect karte hain aur client transfer safe banate hain.

questions.question: Bedsore prevention positioning schedule?

options.A: Prolonged static pressure on bony areas (jaise heel, hips) due to staying in bed in same position for hours

questions.explanation: Bedridden patients me dynamic weight movements zero hone par bony spots par blood circulation stop ho jata hai, jisse bedsores bante hain. Prevention: turn position every 2 hours.

questions.question: Dementia client patient communication style?

options.A: Simple, short sentences use karna, patient tone maintain rakhna aur client ko continuous correct karne se bachna
options.B: Arguing with client over lost memories loudly

questions.explanation: Alzheimer's patients fast speech and corrections se quickly frustrate ho jate hain, short clear statements and validation empathy comfort provide karte hain.

questions.question: Geriatric polypharmacy side effects?

options.A: Single client ko multiple daily prescription daily drugs serve hona (increases side effects and drug interaction risks)

questions.explanation: Polypharmacy (typically 5+ daily drugs) elderly people me organs weakness, confusing behaviors and falls ka major cause banta hai, close medication logs tracking mandatory hai.

questions.question: Elderly home safety fall precautions?

options.A: Remove loose rugs/carpets, install grab bars in toilets and keep floors dry and free of clutter wire pathways
options.B: Placing glossy wax polish on entire floor hamesha
options.C: Keeping rooms dark at night to save electricity

questions.explanation: Falls are leading cause of bone fractures (eg hip fracture) in elderly, floor safety grab-bars installation reduces slip events dramatically.

questions.question: Diabetic foot care checks cuts?

options.A: Check daily for redness, small blisters, cuts or sensory numbness (diabetes slows wound healing, risking gangrene)
options.B: Rinsing feet with hot boiling water directly always
options.C: Only cutting nails to root skins

questions.explanation: Diabetic neuropathy pain signals block karti hai. Foot cuts/blisters unnoticed rehne par severe ulcers construct ho jate hain, daily visual check mandatory checks hai.

questions.question: Elderly dehydration thirst drops?

options.A: Decreases drastically (elderly people feel less thirsty even when body is dry, demanding scheduled hourly water reminders)

questions.explanation: Hypothalamus sensitivity drops with age. Companion scheduled water pushes block urinary tract infections (UTIs) due to dehydration.

questions.question: Wheelchair locks transfers safety?

options.A: Always (unlocked wheels slide backward during patient sitting weight drops, causing tail bone fractures)
options.B: No, wheels must spin free for flexibility

questions.explanation: Securing wheel brakes provides rigid platform stability during weight shifts, preventing slipping accidents.
